## reference · EU AI Act Article 50 — transparency obligations (providers + deployers)

**PROVENANCE NOTE (why this chunk is hand-written):** `night_crawler` fetched the page fine but its
LLM distiller summarised the site NAVIGATION twice — the second pass literally emitted *"The EU AI
Act logo … 846x215 pixels"* — and **both noise distills PASSED the quality guard.** The RAW cache
(`reference_url_44e36a44_article-50-…`, 50,478 chars) contains the full substantive text, so this
distill is written from that verified raw, not from model memory. Lesson: on a nav-dominated page,
check the distill against the raw before trusting it; the link-density warning was the tell.

**The four duties (paraphrased; quotes are verbatim from the cached text):**

1. **Disclose the AI itself.** Providers must ensure AI systems "intended to interact directly with
   natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are
   informed that they are interacting with an AI system" — unless that is obvious to a reasonably
   well-informed, observant and circumspect person.
2. **Mark synthetic output machine-readably.** Providers of systems "generating synthetic audio,
   image, video or text content shall ensure that the outputs of the AI system are **marked in a
   machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated**", with
   solutions that are "effective, interoperable, robust and reliable" as far as technically feasible.
3. **Emotion recognition / biometric categorisation** — deployers must inform the exposed persons.
4. **Deepfakes + public-interest text.** Deployers generating or manipulating image/audio/video
   "constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or
   manipulated"; artistic/satirical works get a lighter disclosure that does not spoil the work.

**WHY THIS MATTERS TO WORKHIVE (candidate dimension, NOT yet encoded).** Our **AI6** dim
(agentic write accountability) makes the DATABASE ROW declare machine authorship. Article 50 asks a
DIFFERENT question: is the **USER TOLD**, and is the **ARTIFACT ITSELF MARKED**? Concretely on this
platform: AI-drafted logbook entries, `alert_detail` guidance, AI reports and the companion's
replies are all AI-generated text a Filipino worker reads — Art. 50(1) is about them KNOWING that,
and 50(2) is about the exported artefact (a PDF report, a shared handover) carrying a
machine-readable marker. **Non-redundancy is NOT yet proven** against AI2 (confidence & grounding),
E3 (trust/provenance) and AI6 — prove or kill it before it earns a class id (§3 discipline; 5
candidates already killed). Scope note: WorkHive is PH-based, so the EU Act is not a legal
obligation here — treat it as a well-specified TRANSPARENCY TAXONOMY, not a compliance mandate.
